blocks:
Ayahs

تَبَـٰرَكَ ٱلَّذِى بِيَدِهِ ٱلْمُلْكُ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱)
Blessed is He in whose hands is all sovereignty, and He has power over all things. (1)
ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلْمَوْتَ وَٱلْحَيَوٰةَ لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْغَفُورُ (۲)
He, who created death and life that He may test you [to see] which of you is best in conduct. And He is the All-mighty, the All-forgiving. (2)

Key Points

In addition to this Surah, Surah Al-Furqan also begins with the word "Tabārak" (Blessed is He).

"Tabārak" (Blessed is He), derived from the root "Barakah" (blessing), means both enduring goodness and high rank. Furthermore, a place where water collects is called a "Birkah" (pool).

What is the purpose of human creation?

Answer: The Qur'an has stated four purposes for the creation of humanity:

A) Worship. We read in the Qur'an: We did not create the jinn and mankind except that they should worship Me. «و ما خلقت الجن والانس الاّ لیعبدون»(2)

B) Choosing the right path from the wrong and achieving growth and perfection under the shadow of divine tests: «خلق الموت و الحیاة لیبلوكم»

C) Mercy upon people. The Qur'an mentions another purpose of creation as the special mercy of Allah upon people, stating: (Humans) were created so that they might be guided to the special mercy of Allah: «الّا من رحم ربّك ولذلك خلقهم»(3)

What are the conditions for a "Hasan" (good) and virtuous deed?

A virtuous deed is one that has been measured and planned in terms of its goal, time, place, necessity, continuity, tools used, collaborators, beneficiaries, steadfastness in the work, firmness, order, foresight in action, required priorities, vitality, moderation, precision, speed, and soundness of the work, and is performed far from the afflictions of conceit ('ujb), nullification (habt), showing off (riyā'), and seeking reputation (sum'ah).

Almighty Allah rules both in this world: «بیده الملك» (In His Hand is the dominion), and He is the Ruler in the Hereafter. «الملك یومئذٍ للّه»(4)

Allah is the sole Ruler: «لم یكن له شریك فى الملك»(5) (He has no partner in the dominion), «بیده الملك» (In His Hand is the dominion), and His rule is accompanied by sanctity. «المَلك القدّوس»(6)

The manifestations of Allah's blessing (Barakah) exist both in creation (takwīn): «تبارك الّذى بیده الملك... خلق الموت و الحیاة» (Blessed is He in Whose Hand is the dominion... Who created death and life), and in legislation (tashrī'): «تبارك الّذى نزّل الفرقان»(7) (Blessed is He Who sent down the Criterion).

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: The intention is not the most work, but rather the best work, and that is performing the deed accompanied by divine awe (khashyah), sincere intention, and virtuousness.(8)

(2) Dhāriyāt, 56.

(3) Hūd, 119.

(4) Hajj, 76.

(5) Furqān, 2.

(6) Jumu‘ah, 1.

(7) Furqān, 1.

(8) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 16.

Messages

1- Although the Qur'an has used the word "Tabārak" regarding itself and the Prophet upon whom it was revealed, the source of all blessings is Allah and His power. «تبارك الّذى بیده الملك و هو على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

2- All powers and governments are perishable; only the dominion of Allah is eternal. «بیده الملك و هو على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

3- Worldly governments are partial, temporary, and limited, and thus do not possess widespread blessing (Barakah). «تبارك الّذى بیده الملك»

4- The decree of death and life is a manifestation of the absolute sovereignty of Allah. «بیده الملك... خلق الموت و الحیاة»

5- Worldly rulers do not have power over all affairs, but Allah is the Ruler and is Capable of all things. «بیده الملك و هو على كلّ شى ءٍ قدیر»

6- Death does not mean annihilation and destruction; rather, it is an existential matter that has been created, representing the transition from this world to the next. «خلق الموت»

7- The creation of death is a test for the manifestation of patience, and the creation of life is a test for the manifestation of gratitude. «خلق الموت والحیاة لیبلوكم»

8- All creations are purposeful, and nothing has been created in vain. Allah created humanity for selective perfection. «لیبلوكم»

9- The essence of humanity is revealed amidst hardships and the bitter and sweet experiences of life. «خلق الموت والحیاة لیبلوكم»

10- The divine test is not to ascertain Allah's knowledge, for He knows everything beforehand; rather, it is so that an action may proceed from us. «لیبلوكم ایّكم احسن عملاً» (Like a teacher who knows the student's knowledge but tests him so that the student's deserving of the grade may become manifest.)

11- The criterion for action is quality, not quantity. «أحسن عملاً» (The best deed, not «اكثر عملاً» the most deed).

12- The divine test is continuous and for everyone. «لیبلوكم» (This indicates continuity).

13- We should not be satisfied with merely doing good work; the best work must be performed. «احسن عملاً»

14- Only the dominion and power of Allah are invincible. «بیده الملك... قدیر... هو العزیز»

15- The might and power of Allah are accompanied by compassion and mercy. «العزیز الغفور»

16- Do not despair over failure in divine tests, for He is the Forgiving. «الغفور»

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah Al-Mulk Surah Al-Mulk is the opening Surah of the twenty-ninth Juz’ of the Holy Qur'an. This Surah is one of the Meccan Surahs and contains thirty Ayahs. The other name for this Surah is "Tabārak." "Mulk" and "Tabārak" are both derived from the first Ayah and mean "Sovereignty" and "Full of Blessing," respectively.

The content of this Surah generally includes the following three axes: 1. Discussions concerning the origin of existence, the attributes of Allah, the wondrous system of creation, the creation of existence and humanity, and the tools humans possess for knowledge. 2. Discussions about the Resurrection (Ma‘ād), the torment of Hell, and the conversation of the inhabitants of Hell on the Day of Judgment. 3. Warning the disbelievers and oppressors of worldly and otherworldly punishments.

Regarding the virtue of this Surah, it has been stated: Reciting this Surah leads to salvation from the torment of the grave.(1)

(1) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَمَـٰوَٰتٍ طِبَاقًا ۖ مَّا تَرَىٰ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ مِن تَفَـٰوُتٍ ۖ فَٱرْجِعِ ٱلْبَصَرَ هَلْ تَرَىٰ مِن فُطُورٍ (۳)
He created seven heavens in layers. You do not see any discordance in the creation of the All-beneficent. Look again! Do you see any flaw? (3)
ثُمَّ ٱرْجِعِ ٱلْبَصَرَ كَرَّتَيْنِ يَنقَلِبْ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْبَصَرُ خَاسِئًا وَهُوَ حَسِيرٌ (٤)
Look again, once more. Your look will return to you humbled and weary. (4)

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ زَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِمَصَـٰبِيحَ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهَا رُجُومًا لِّلشَّيَـٰطِينِ ۖ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لَهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلسَّعِيرِ (٥)
We have certainly adorned the lowest heaven with lamps, and made them [the means of pelting] missiles against the devils, and We have prepared for them a punishment of the Blaze. (5)

Key Points

«طِباق» is either the verbal noun (maṣdar) of «طابق» or the plural of «طَبَق». In the first case, «سبع سماوات طباقا» means that the heavens are harmonious and corresponding to one another. In the second case, it means that the heavens are seven layers one above the other. «تفاوت» (disparity) is derived from «فوت» (loss/failure) meaning the occurrence of disorder and disharmony, and «فُطور» means a fissure or crack along the length. «خاسئاً» means weary and unsuccessful, and «حسیر» means powerless or exhausted. Bitterness and shortcomings appear unpleasant to superficial people, otherwise researchers look upon everything with a positive view. Sourness and pepper are unpleasant for a child, but for their parents, sourness is as valuable as jam. «مصباح» (lamp/light), derived from «صبح» (morning), is a device that illuminates the night like the morning, such as a lamp. In these verses, three expressions are used regarding the stars of the heaven: 1. Miṣbāḥ (lamp) and light; 2. Adornment and splendor (Zīnat); 3. Stoning (Rajm) of the devils. We read in verses 7 and 8 of Sūrat Aṣ-Ṣāffāt that the devils intend to infiltrate the heaven to eavesdrop and listen to celestial news, but they are targeted and driven away every time, and thus the stars are a means of protecting the heaven.

Messages

1- The system of creation is based on the Mercy of Allah. «فى خلق الرّحمن»

2- The existing system is the best system (niẓām aḥsan), and there is no flaw or deficiency in the system of creation. «ماترى فى خلق الرّحمن من تفاوت»

3- Knowledge of God must be based on observation and precision, so that human faith in the grandeur of existence and the power of the Creator may increase. «فارجع البصر... ثمّ ارجع البصر»

4- To acquire knowledge, seeing and observing once is not enough. «فارجع البصر... ثمّ ارجع البصر كرّتین» (In this verse, the word «ترى» (you see) is repeated twice, and the word «بصر» (sight) is repeated three times along with the phrase «كرّتین» (twice/repeatedly).)

5- The God Who has performed His work firmly and wisely is not worried about the scrutiny of others; rather, He invites others to inspect it. «فارجع البصر... ثم ارجع البصر»

6- Allah does not tire from creating: «و ما مسّنا من لغوب» (9) but our sight tires from looking at His creations. «ینقلب الیك البصر خاسئاً و هو حسیر»

7- Adornment (Zīnat) is one of the innate desires of humanity. (One of the blessings of the stars is counted as their being an adornment. «زیّنا السّماء الدنیا»)

8- Divine creations, despite their firmness and strength, «سبعاً شداداً» (10) possess a special adornment. (In architecture, besides the structural integrity of the building, the beauty of the facade must also be considered.) «زیّنا السّماء الدنیا»

9- Divine creations have various applications. «مصابیح - رجوماً»

10- One should not react passively against the enemy; our confrontation with the enemy must be aggressive. «رجوماً للشیاطینً»

11- The devils covet the heavens as well. «رجوماً للشیاطین»

12- The devils are vulnerable to attack. «رجوماً للشیاطین»

9) Qāf, 38.

10) An-Naba’, 12.

Ayahs

وَلِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ عَذَابُ جَهَنَّمَ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (٦)
For those who defy their Lord is the punishment of hell, and it is an evil destination. (6)
إِذَآ أُلْقُوا۟ فِيهَا سَمِعُوا۟ لَهَا شَهِيقًا وَهِىَ تَفُورُ (۷)
When they are thrown in it, they hear it blaring, as it seethes, (7)
تَكَادُ تَمَيَّزُ مِنَ ٱلْغَيْظِ ۖ كُلَّمَآ أُلْقِىَ فِيهَا فَوْجٌ سَأَلَهُمْ خَزَنَتُهَآ أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ نَذِيرٌ (۸)
almost exploding with rage. Whenever a group is thrown in it, its keepers will ask them, ‘Did not any warner come to you?’ (8)

Key Points

Uliqū (اُلقوا) is derived from ilqā' (القاء), meaning to be thrown without choice. Shahīq (شَهیق) refers to the ugliest and most unpleasant sounds, like the grating braying sound of donkeys. Tafūru (تَفور), derived from fawarān (fountain/boiling), indicates the massive flames of Hell that boil and rise up. The unpleasant sound of shahīq comes from Hell itself, (lahā shahīqan - لها شهیقا), and the inhabitants of Hell also have shahīq, as we read in Ayah 106 of Surah Hūd: «لهم فیها زفیر و شهیق».

Messages

1- Wrath and rage sometimes reach the point of explosion. «تكاد تمیّز من الغیظ»

2- The inhabitants of Hell are thrown into the fire. «القوا فیها»

3- The punishment of Hell is accompanied by humiliation: «القوا فیها», it is grating: «سمعوا لها شهیقا», and it is accompanied by a question of reproach. «سئلهم خزنتها»

4- Divine punishment follows the completion of the argument (proof). «الم یأتكم نذیر»

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ قَدْ جَآءَنَا نَذِيرٌ فَكَذَّبْنَا وَقُلْنَا مَا نَزَّلَ ٱللَّهُ مِن شَىْءٍ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ كَبِيرٍ (۹)
They will say, ‘Yes, a warner did come to us, but we impugned [him] and said, ‘Allah did not send down anything; you are only in great error.’ (9)
وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْ كُنَّا نَسْمَعُ أَوْ نَعْقِلُ مَا كُنَّا فِىٓ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ (۱٠)
And they will say, ‘Had we listened or applied reason, we would not have been among inmates of the Blaze.’ (10)
فَٱعْتَرَفُوا۟ بِذَنۢبِهِمْ فَسُحْقًا لِّأَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ (۱۱)
Thus they will admit their sin. So away with the inmates of the Blaze! (11)

Key Points

In these three verses, three confessions of the disbelievers on the Day of Judgment are mentioned: A) Confession of the coming of the prophets and their denial (of them). «بلى قد جاءنا نذیر فكذّبنا» B) Confession of lacking intellect and failing to listen to the truth. «لو كنّا نسمع او نعقل» C) Confession of committing sin. «فاعترفوا بذنبهم» In Ayah 26 of Surah Fussilat, we read that the opponents used to say: «لا تسمعوا لهذا القرآن» (Do not listen to this Qur'an). But on that Day, they will say: If only we had listened! «لو كنّا نسمع»

Messages

1- Allah, Whose goal is the spiritual perfection of man, must send a heavenly leader; otherwise, this goal will not be realized. «قالوا بلى قد جاءنا نذیر»

2- The result of deliberate and conscious denial of religion is Hellfire. «القوا فیها... جاءنا نذیر فكذّبنا»

3- The transgressor justifies his actions. (To justify their denial, the disbelievers used to say: Allah has not revealed anything at all.) «فكذّبنا وقلنا ما نزّل اللّه من شى ء»

4- The root of denial is the lack of intellect/reasoning. «كذّبنا... لو كنّا نسمع او نعقل» (The teachings of Islam are consistent with reason; if we had reasoned, we would have become believers.)

5- True intellect is that which enables a person to hear the truth, accept it, and follow it, thereby saving himself from Divine wrath. «لو كنّا نسمع او نعقل ما كُنّا فى اصحاب السّعیر»

6- The spirit of obstinacy reaches a point where the misguided attribute misguidance—and great misguidance at that!—to the Divine Prophets. «ان انتم الاّ فى ضلال كبیر»

7- Confession of sin in this world may lead to forgiveness, but never in the Hereafter. «فاعترفوا بذنبهم... اصحاب السّعیر»

8- On the Day of Judgment, the disbelievers will suffer both physical torment: «اصحاب السعیر» (Companions of the Blaze), and spiritual torment. «سُحقاً» (Being far removed from Divine Mercy).

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلْغَيْبِ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ (۱۲)
Indeed for those who fear their Lord in secret there will be forgiveness and a great reward. (12)
وَأَسِرُّوا۟ قَوْلَكُمْ أَوِ ٱجْهَرُوا۟ بِهِۦٓ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (۱۳)
Speak secretly, or do so openly, indeed He knows well what is in the breasts. (13)
أَلَا يَعْلَمُ مَنْ خَلَقَ وَهُوَ ٱللَّطِيفُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ (۱٤)
Would He who has created not know? And He is the All-attentive, the All-aware. (14)

Key Points

| According to a narration in Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn, Imam Ridha عليه السلام said: The meaning of Latif (Subtle) is not smallness or delicacy, but rather that the knowledge of Allah penetrates all things, yet His comprehension by others is impossible.

Messages

  1. In the method of education, the reprimand of the wicked and the encouragement of the good must be placed side by side so that they can be compared. (The previous verses spoke of the punishment of the dwellers of Hell, and this verse speaks of the dwellers of Paradise.) «انّ الّذین یخشون ربّهم»

  2. Awe (Khashyah) of Allah is effective only when it is continuous. «یخشون» (yakhshawna) is a present tense verb and indicates continuity.

  3. Purification from sins is the prerequisite for receiving divine favors. «لهم مغفرة و اجر كبیر»

  4. True piety (Taqwa) and awe (Khashyah) are when a person fears Allah even within his own self; otherwise, apparent piety is nothing more than mere pretense. «یخشون ربّهم بالغیب»

  5. Let us abandon hypocrisy and dissimulation, for Allah is aware of inner realities. «علیم بذات الصدور»

  6. Concealment has no effect on the profound knowledge of Allah. He knows all intentions and the secrets of hearts. «انّه علیم بذات الصدور» («علیم» [Aleem] indicates deep and extensive knowledge.)

  7. Allah's knowledge of His creatures stems from His quality as the Creator. (He who has made and created something is aware of the conditions of His creation.) «الا یعلم من خلق»

  8. Belief in Allah's knowledge is the best deterrent against hypocrisy and secrecy. «اسرّوا قولكم اواجهروا به... الا یعلم...»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ ذَلُولًا فَٱمْشُوا۟ فِى مَنَاكِبِهَا وَكُلُوا۟ مِن رِّزْقِهِۦ ۖ وَإِلَيْهِ ٱلنُّشُورُ (۱٥)
It is He who made the earth tractable for you; so walk on its flanks and eat of His provision, and towards Him is the resurrection. (15)

Key Points

«مَنكب» means shoulder, and the shoulder is the best limb for placing a load upon it. The shoulder of the earth means the parts of the earth upon which the burden of your sustenance (rizq) rests. The earth is subservient to man, and despite several types of movement, it is tranquil. If the earth were constantly afflicted by earthquakes or volcanoes, or if the distance between the earth and the sun were farther or closer, or if any of the existing calculations governing the earth were absent, the earth would not be subservient to man. «جعل لكم الارض ذلولاً»

Messages

1- Allah has made the earth subservient so that the possibility for human effort and striving upon it is provided. «جعل لكم الارض ذلولاً فامشوا فى مناكبها»

2- Sustenance (rizq) is from Allah, but effort is necessary to obtain it. «فامشوا فى مناكبها و كلوا من رزقه»

3- Existence is in motion and moving towards perfection:

Nature for man. «جعل لكم الارض ذلولاً»

Man for effort. «فامشوا فى مناكبها»

Effort for sustenance. «كلوا من رزقه»

The world for the Hereafter. «الیه النشور»

4- Worldly enjoyments should not cause heedlessness regarding the Day of Resurrection. «كلوا... و الیه النشور»

Ayahs

ءَأَمِنتُم مَّن فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ أَن يَخْسِفَ بِكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ فَإِذَا هِىَ تَمُورُ (۱٦)
Are you secure that He who is in the sky will not make the earth swallow you while it quakes? (16)
أَمْ أَمِنتُم مَّن فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ أَن يُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ حَاصِبًا ۖ فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ كَيْفَ نَذِيرِ (۱۷)
Are you secure that He who is in the sky will not unleash upon you a rain of stones? Soon you will know how My warning has been! (17)

Key Points

The content of these two verses is similar to Ayah 65 of Surah Al-An'am, which states: Tell the people: He is capable of sending down a punishment upon you from the sky or from the earth. «قل هو القادر على ان یبعث علیكم عذاباً من فوقكم او من تحت ارجلكم»

Hāsib (حاصب) means a strong wind that carries gravel (or pebbles).

We read the example of sinking into the earth concerning Qarun at the end of Surah Al-Qasas, where He says: «فخسفنا به» (So We caused the earth to swallow him). And we read the example of gravel descending from the sky in the story of the people of Lot, where He said: «انّا ارسلنا علیهم حاصباً»(11)

Allah's power in granting tranquility to the earth is a manifestation of His sovereignty, which we read at the beginning of the Surah: «بیده الملك و هو على كلّ شى ءٍ قدیر»(12)

The possibility of change and upheaval in existence saves man from becoming arrogant and relying solely on the world. Mowlavi (Rumi) has included a beautiful poem about this in the Mathnawi:

All particles of earth and sky Are the army of the Truth (Allah) during trial. Did you see what the wind did to the 'Ad? Did you see what the water did in the flood? What that sea of vengeance struck upon Pharaoh, And what this earth showed to Qarun, And what the Ababil birds did to the elephant, And what the mosquito consumed of Nimrod's head, And when David threw the stone by hand, It shattered into three hundred pieces and the army was defeated. If I speak of the inanimate objects of the world, Wisely aiding the Prophets, The Mathnawi would become so vast that forty camels, If they carried it, would be overwhelmed by the heavy load.

11) Qamar, 34. 12) Mulk, 1.

Messages

1- Allah's wrath and punishment are not limited only to the Day of Judgment. «ءامنتم... أن یخسف بكم الارض»

2- No one should consider himself safe from Allah's wrath, and the possibility of punishment descending exists at every moment. «ءامنتم من فى السماء»

3- Heaven (the sky) is the center of Divine command and administration. «من فى السماء»

4- Heaven (the sky) is the center of obedient agents under Allah's command. «من فى السماء»

5- The system governing nature is by His Will, and He can change it at any moment. «یخسف بكم الارض»

6- Divine wrath may come from beneath man's feet and cause him to sink into the earth. «یخسف بكم الارض» Or it may descend from above his head, from the sky. «یرسل علیكم حاصباً»

7- Allah's Hand is open (His power is limitless). He can both send abundant rain from the sky: «یرسل السماء علیم مدراراً»(13) and He can rain down stones from the sky. «یرسل علیكم حاصباً»

8- We must take Divine warnings seriously. «فستعلمون كیف نذیر»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ (۱۸)
Certainly those who were before them had impugned [My apostles]; but then how was My rebuttal! (18)
أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ إِلَى ٱلطَّيْرِ فَوْقَهُمْ صَـٰٓفَّـٰتٍ وَيَقْبِضْنَ ۚ مَا يُمْسِكُهُنَّ إِلَّا ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍۭ بَصِيرٌ (۱۹)
Have they not regarded the birds above them spreading and closing their wings? No one sustains them except the All-beneficent. Indeed, He watches all things. (19)

Key Points

Birds, possessing collective movements and traversing long distances across four seasons, with continuous landings and flights without spatial limitations, yet not falling or colliding with each other, are signs of divine greatness and power. The word ṣāffāt (صافّات) is used in the active participle form, meaning the bird flies with its wings spread open. However, the word yaqbiḍhunna (یقبضهنّ) is used in the verb form, because the bird sometimes folds its wings.

Messages

1- We must take Allah's threats seriously. (Allah's wrath was carried out against previous nations; Qarun sank into the earth, and stones rained down upon the people of Lot from the sky. Therefore, you should learn lessons from their history.) «فكیف كان نكیر»

2- Allah consoles His Prophet against the people's denial. «لقد كذّب الّذین من قبلهم»

3- Past history is a guiding light for future generations. «كذّب الّذین من قبلهم»

4- Denying the prophets is the key to divine wrath. «كذّب... فكیف كان نكیر»

5- Material means should not make you arrogant. (Although the bird flies by flapping its wings, what holds it in the air is the power of Allah.) «ما یمسكهنّ الاّ الرّحمن»

6- Do not associate anything as a partner with Allah. «الاّ الرّحمن»

7- Divine blessings are based on Mercy. «ما یمسكهنّ الاّ الرّحمن»

8- The universal order is managed under the supervision of Allah. «انّه بكلّ شى ء بصیر»

Ayahs

أَمَّنْ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى هُوَ جُندٌ لَّكُمْ يَنصُرُكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ۚ إِنِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ إِلَّا فِى غُرُورٍ (۲٠)
Who is it that is your host who may help you, besides the All-beneficent? The faithless only dwell in delusion. (20)
أَمَّنْ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى يَرْزُقُكُمْ إِنْ أَمْسَكَ رِزْقَهُۥ ۚ بَل لَّجُّوا۟ فِى عُتُوٍّ وَنُفُورٍ (۲۱)
Who is it that may provide for you if He withholds His provision? Indeed, they persist in defiance and aversion. (21)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the discussion was about Divine power in the earth and the heavens. He can command the earth to swallow the disbelievers, He can hold the birds in the sky, and so on. This verse highlights the helplessness and weakness of humanity, questioning upon which army and with what support this human being shows so much obstinacy against Allah. It seems the polytheists felt they derived strength from their idols. Gharūr (غَرور) means deceiver, and Ghurūr (غُرور) means deception (or delusion). The tools of delusion are varied, and the agent of it is Satan: «لایغرّنّكم باللّه الغرور»(14) Long hopes and misleading promises are means of delusion. Here, the factors leading to human misery are mentioned: delusion (or arrogance), obstinacy, rebellion, and fleeing from the truth. ‘Utuww (عُتوّ) means arrogance and transgression against the truth, and Nufūr (نفور) means expressing aversion and fleeing from the truth. Obstinacy is the ground for rebellion and transgression, and rebellion is the ground for aversion and distancing oneself from the truth. Only Allah is the Provider. He provides sustenance in this world: «خلقكم ثمّ رزقكم»(15) He provides sustenance in the Barzakh (intermediate realm): «بل احیاءٌ عند ربّهم یرزقون»(16) And He provides sustenance on the Day of Judgment: «یرزقون فیها بغیر حساب»(17)

13) Nūḥ, 11. 14) Luqmān, 33. 15) Rūm, 40. 16) Āl ‘Imrān, 169. 17) Ghāfir, 40.

Messages

1- The arrogant person must be made aware of his weaknesses and incapacities. «امّن هذا الّذى...»

2- One of the tools of delusion is possessing an army and troops. «جُند لكم ینصركم... الاّ فى غرور»

3- Disbelievers fall into delusion and deception by relying on flimsy powers. «جند لكم ینصركم... ان الكافرون الاّ فى غرور»

4- Allah is not compelled to provide sustenance; rather, He can withhold provision. «ان امسك رزقه»

5- One way to appreciate Allah’s blessings is to consider the possibility of those blessings being withdrawn. «ان امسك رزقه» (In another verse of this same Surah, He says: «ان اصبح ماءكم غورا»(18) If your water were to sink deep into the earth, and you had no access to it.) 18) Al-Mulk, 30.

Ayahs

أَفَمَن يَمْشِى مُكِبًّا عَلَىٰ وَجْهِهِۦٓ أَهْدَىٰٓ أَمَّن يَمْشِى سَوِيًّا عَلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۲۲)
Is he who walks prone on his face better guided, or he who walks upright on a straight path? (22)
قُلْ هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنشَأَكُمْ وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ وَٱلْأَفْـِٔدَةَ ۖ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَشْكُرُونَ (۲۳)
Say, ‘It is He who created you, and made for you hearing, eyesight, and hearts. Little do you thank.’ (23)
قُلْ هُوَ ٱلَّذِى ذَرَأَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَإِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ (۲٤)
Say, ‘It is He who created you on the earth, and toward Him you will be mustered.’ (24)

Key Points

Mukkabb (مُكبّ) means someone who has fallen on his face. Inshā' (انشاء) means creation accompanied by innovation, and dhar'a (ذرأ) means creation and multiplication of offspring. In this Ayah, the disbeliever is likened to someone who has fallen face down on the ground and tries to move by stumbling and rising, while the believer is likened to someone who walks upright. Sam‘ (سمع - hearing) is a verbal noun (masdar) and includes both singular and plural, thus there is no need to bring it in the plural form, unlike absār (ابصار - sights) and af'idah (افئده - hearts) which are brought in the plural form. Someone who has fallen face down has no vision, moves slowly and with difficulty because he does not see the obstacles in the path, his hands and face are injured, he becomes humiliated and disgraced, others move forward while he remains alone. But someone who walks standing upright is honored, moves quickly and swiftly, identifies and examines obstacles from afar, thus remaining safe, and others accompany him.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The heart is of four types; one of them is the inverted heart, which is the heart of the polytheist. Then He recited this Ayah: «أفمن یمشى مكبّاً على وجهه»(19)

Self-knowledge is a path to knowing Allah. Hadrat Ali عليه السلام says: The ear hears with bone, and the eye sees with fat. «جعل لكم السمع و الابصار»

Although our gratitude in return for Allah's blessings is very little, Allah does not withhold His blessings from us. In the supplication of the month of Rajab, we read: «یا من یعطى الكثیر بالقلیل» O God, Who grants much in return for little action and gratitude.

19) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 423.

Messages

1- Obstinate individuals who fight and flee from the truth are, in a sense, transformed, and instead of natural movement, they move like crawling creatures. «بل لجّوا فى عُتوٍّ و نُفور... مُكبّاً على وجهه»

2- The path of Islam is smooth, straight, and has a clear destination, and if the Islamic Ummah follows this path, it will be upright and proud. «یمشى سویّاً على صراط مستقیم»

3- The creation of man is highly important, such that the Prophet is commanded to repeatedly warn people about the origin of their existence and the blessings hidden therein. «قل هو الّذى انشأكم... قل هو الّذى ذرأكم»

4- Allah's creation is innovative, not a simulation based on a previous model. «انشأكم»

5- God granted man all the tools of recognition so that He could complete the argument against him. (Ordinary humans only hear, see, and take heed, but superior humans receive matters through the heart.) «جعل لكم السمع و الابصار و الافئدة»

6- Gratitude to Allah is not only verbal; rather, practical gratitude, which is the correct utilization of blessings, is necessary. «قلیلاً ما تشكرون»

7- He Who created you on earth and from the earth at the beginning of creation also has the power to resurrect you again. «انشأكم، ذرأكم... الیه تحشرون»

8- Allah disperses humans on earth in this world and gathers them on the Day of Judgment. «ذرأكم، تحشرون»

Ayahs

وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۲٥)
They say, ‘When will this promise be fulfilled, if you are truthful?’ (25)
قُلْ إِنَّمَا ٱلْعِلْمُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَإِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۲٦)
Say, ‘Its knowledge is only with Allah; I am only a manifest warner.’ (26)

Key Points

In the Quran, the phrase, «متى هذا الوعد» (When is this promise?) has appeared six times on the tongues of the opponents, and the Prophet would reply to them: Knowledge of the Hour (Resurrection) is only with Allah. Knowledge of the unseen (Ghayb) is of two types: one type that Allah grants to His chosen ones: «تلك من انباء الغیب نوحیها الیك»(20) meaning, what We have related are among the tidings of the unseen which We reveal to you. And a type that is exclusive to Allah alone, and even the Divine Prophets are not aware of it, such as the knowledge of the time the Hour will be established: «انّما العلم عنداللّه» (The knowledge is only with Allah). Just as we read in supplication: «بحق علمك الّذى استأثرت به لنفسك» (By the right of Your knowledge which You have kept exclusively for Yourself). Not knowing the details and the time of the occurrence of the Hour is not a reason to deny its general reality. If the sound of the doorbell rings and you do not see the person ringing it, that is not a reason to say that no one is behind the door.

(20) Hud, 49.

Messages

1- The disbeliever seeks excuses. (Asking about the time of the occurrence of the Hour is an evasion of the principle of the Hour itself.) «متى هذا الوعد»

2- The enemy seeks to weaken the school of thought (Maktab) and the heavenly leader through posing questions, humiliation, and mockery; hence, they accuse the Prophets of lying. «متى هذا الوعد ان كنتم صادقین»

3- Not every piece of knowledge is valuable. (If we knew the time of the Hour, it would cause fear and anxiety for the group closer to that time, and it would cause negligence and forgetfulness for the group that has a long distance from it.) «انّما العلم عنداللّه»

4- The mission of the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is not to give tidings of the unseen (Ghayb), but rather guidance and warning. «انّما انا نذیر مبین»

5- The speech of the Prophets is clear, without ambiguity or complexity. «نذیر مبین»

6- Arrogant and stubborn individuals must be guided through the method of warning, and glad tidings and encouragement are not effective for such people. «نذیر مبین»

Ayah

فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهُ زُلْفَةً سِيٓـَٔتْ وُجُوهُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَقِيلَ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَدَّعُونَ (۲۷)
When they see it brought near, the countenances of the faithless will be contorted, and [they will be] told, ‘This is what you were asking for!’ (27)

Key Points

Zulfah (زلفة) means near. The land of Mash'ar al-Haram is called Muzdalifah (مزدلفة) because it is near Mecca. Tadda'ūn (تدّعون) means a serious and hasty request. In Ayah 14 of Surah Adh-Dhariyat, we read: «یوم هم على النّار یفتنون ذوقوا فتنتكم هذا الّذى كنتم به تستعجلون»(21) (Recall the day when they are tormented and punished by the Fire, [and it is said:] Taste your torment; this is what you sought hastily and used to say: «ایّان یوم الدّین»(22)).

The Holy Quran describes the appearance of the righteous and the criminals on the Day of Judgment in several verses. Regarding the righteous, He says: «وجوهٌ یومئذٍ مسفرة ضاحكةٌ مستبشرة»(23) (On that Day, faces will be shining, laughing, and joyful.) «وجوهٌ یومئذٍ ناعمة»(24) (Faces on that Day will be fresh and vibrant.) «تعرف فى وجوههم نضرة النعیم»(25) (You will recognize the freshness and vibrancy of bliss in their faces.)

But regarding the deviants and criminals, He says: «و وجوهٌ یومئذٍ علیها غبرة ترهقها قترة»(26) (On that Day, faces will be covered with dust and gloom, and darkness and blackness will envelop them.) «وجوهٌ یومئذٍ خاشعة»(27) (Faces on that Day will be humbled and downcast.) «تلفح وجوههم النار و هم فیها كالحون»(28) (The flames of the Fire will severely scorch their faces, and their faces will be grim and distorted, their teeth exposed due to the burning of their lips.) «و نحشرهم یوم القیامة على وجوههم عمیاً و بكماً و صمّاً»(29) (We shall gather them on the Day of Resurrection, blind, dumb, and deaf, upon their faces.) «وجوهٌ یومئذٍ باسرة»(30) (Faces on that Day will be severely grim and contracted.) «یوم یسحبون فى النار على وجوههم»(31) (The Day they are dragged upon their faces into the Fire.)

21) Adh-Dhariyat, 13 - 14. 22) Adh-Dhariyat, 12. 23) Abasa, 38 - 39. 24) Al-Ghashiyah, 8. 25) Al-Mutaffifin, 24. 26) Abasa, 40 - 41. 27) Al-Ghashiyah, 2. 28) Al-Mu'minun, 104. 29) Al-Isra, 97. 30) Al-Qiyamah, 24. 31) Al-Qamar, 48.

Messages

1- Disbelief (Kufr) in this world causes blackness of face (disgrace) in the Hereafter. «سیئت وجوه الّذین كفروا...»

2- On the Day of Judgment, in addition to physical torment, there is also the spiritual torment of humiliation. «هذا الّذى كنتم به تدّعون»

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَهْلَكَنِىَ ٱللَّهُ وَمَن مَّعِىَ أَوْ رَحِمَنَا فَمَن يُجِيرُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (۲۸)
Say, ‘Tell me, whether Allah destroys me and those who are with me, or He has mercy on us, who will shelter the faithless from a painful punishment?’ (28)

Key Points

We read in narrations that the disbelievers of Mecca wished for the death of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and the Muslims. This Ayah states: and assuming they pass away, who will protect you from the wrath of Allah? This wish is also mentioned in Surah At-Tur: «ام یقولون شاعر نتربّص به ریب المنون»(32) They say: Muhammad is a poet for whom we await bitter events and death. In Ayah 12 of Surah Al-Fath, this wish of the disbelievers is also mentioned: that the Prophet and the believers would not return safely from the battlefield. «بل ظننتم ان لن ینقلب الرسول والمؤمنون الى اهلیهم» It is said that a truant child asked Allah for the death of his teacher. His father told him: If you want to be relieved from studying and lessons, you should ask Allah for my death, because if your teacher dies, I will send you to another teacher.

32) At-Tur, 30

Messages

1- Let us learn the method of argumentation and dialogue with the disbelievers from the revelation. «قل ارایتم...»

2- The Prophet is the trustee of the revelation. «قل...», (He does not even omit the word "Say" [قل].)

3- In the method of propagation, sometimes truths must be presented in the form of a hypothesis. «ان اهلكنى اللّه»

4- Just as the disbelievers harbor hatred and malice toward the person of the Prophet, they also harbor malice toward his followers. «اهلكنى اللّه و من معى»

5- A believer must live between fear and hope. «اهلكنى اللّه... او رحمنا»

6- Nothing and no one can prevent the descent of Divine wrath upon the disbelievers. «فمن یجیر الكافرین من عذاب الیم» (The slogan of some disbelievers was «و ما نحن بمعذّبین»(33), meaning we will not be afflicted by punishment, but the Quran states that they will be afflicted by punishment.)

7- Whoever wishes pain and destruction for the friends (Awliya) of Allah will be afflicted by a painful punishment. «عذاب الیم» 33) Ash-Shu'ara, 138.

Ayahs

قُلْ هُوَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ وَعَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْنَا ۖ فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ هُوَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۲۹)
Say, ‘He is the All-beneficent; we have faith in Him, and in Him do we trust. Soon you will know who is in manifest error.’ (29)
قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَصْبَحَ مَآؤُكُمْ غَوْرًا فَمَن يَأْتِيكُم بِمَآءٍ مَّعِينٍۭ (۳٠)
Say, ‘Tell me, should your water sink down [into the ground], who will bring you running water?’ (30)

Key Points

The earth is composed of permeable and impermeable sections. If all parts of it were impermeable, no water would be stored, and if the entire earth were permeable, the ground would turn into a swamp. Ghawr (غَور) means sinking into the depth, and Ma'īn (مَعین) means easy and flowing. The last Ayah serves as an interpretation of the preceding Ayah. In Ayah 29, He says: «قل هو الرحمن» (Say: He is the Most Compassionate), and in Ayah 30, He mentions flowing water as an example of that compassion (mercy). In the first Ayah, He says: We have relied upon Him, and in the next Ayah, He says: The reason for relying upon Him is that if the needed water sinks into the earth, no one can bring it back. Imam Baqir عليه السلام (in the esoteric interpretation of the Ayah) said: If your Imam is absent, who will send you a manifest Imam to tell you the news of the heavens and the earth, and the lawful and the unlawful? «ان اصبح ماءكم غوراً...»(34)

34) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The Prophet has the duty to declare his position regarding the unworthy. «قل هو الرّحمن»

2- Against the disbelievers, two factors—faith and reliance (Tawakkul)—are your means of salvation. «آمنّا... توكّلنا»

3- Reliance (Tawakkul) is the fruit of faith and inseparable from it. «آمنّا... توكّلنا»

4- Faith is valuable when directed toward the source of Mercy, not toward inanimate idols. «هو الرّحمن آمنّا»

5- By relying upon Allah, the disbelievers will take their hopes to the grave. «علیه توكلنا فستعلمون...»

6- The end of the matter is the criterion, not its beginning. «فستعلمون من هو فى ضلال مبین»

7- First, strengthen your ideological foundation, and then proceed to threaten the enemy. «آمنّا... توكّلنا... فستعلمون...»

8- The decisive and firm speech of the leader of the Islamic community causes reassurance among the insiders and anxiety among the outsiders. «قل هو الرّحمن... فستعلمون...»

9- The possibility of danger is sufficient to prevent indifference and negligence. «اِن اصبح ماءكم غوراً»

10- The Hand of Allah is open to change the laws of existence. «اِن اصبح ماءكم غوراً»
